## Accuracy trap on multiclass imbalance

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-majority
#| echo: true
#| message: false
majority_label <- peng_imb3 |>
  dplyr::count(y3, sort = TRUE) |>
  dplyr::slice(1) |>
  dplyr::pull(y3) |>
  as.character()

tibble(
  strategy = paste("Always predict", majority_label),
  accuracy_on_peng_imb3 = mean(as.character(peng_imb3$y3) == majority_label)
)
```

- High overall accuracy can still hide poor minority detection.
- For multiclass imbalance, report **macro recall**, **macro F1**, and **per-class recall**.

## Holdout confusion matrices (same split)

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-holdout-no
#| echo: true
#| message: false
set.seed(13)
split_imb3 <- initial_split(peng_imb3, prop = 0.8, strata = y3)
fit_imb3 <- fit(wf_imb3, training(split_imb3))
pred_imb3 <- augment(fit_imb3, testing(split_imb3))

pred_imb3 |>
  conf_mat(truth = y3, estimate = .pred_class)
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-holdout-up
#| echo: true
#| message: false
fit_imb3_up <- fit(wf_imb3_up, training(split_imb3))
pred_imb3_up <- augment(fit_imb3_up, testing(split_imb3))

pred_imb3_up |>
  conf_mat(truth = y3, estimate = .pred_class)
```

## Per-class recall comparison

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-recall-kable
#| echo: false
bind_rows(
  multiclass_recall_table(pred_imb3, truth_col = "y3") |> mutate(model = "No upsample"),
  multiclass_recall_table(pred_imb3_up, truth_col = "y3") |> mutate(model = "Upsample")
) |>
  mutate(recall = round(recall, 3)) |>
  select(model, truth, recall) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = model, values_from = recall) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Per-class recall (holdout)")
```

Upsampling improves the underrepresented class only if it increases minority recall at acceptable tradeoff.
